title: MBTA announces schedule changes, detours as nearly 1 million expected to attend Sunday’s St. Patrick’s Day parade
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: MBTA modifies schedules for St. Patrick's Day parade in South Boston, with nearly one million attendees expected.
Red Line operates on 'rush hour service' schedule from 10 a.m. to 6 p.m.; Broadway stop may be bypassed.
Shuttle buses run from South Station to City Point; buses 9 and 11 are suspended, and several routes face detours.

title: Boston Public Schools graduation rate reached all-time high last year, still lags behind state average
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston Public Schools' graduation rate hit a record 81.3% in 2025, up 1.6 points from the previous year.
Despite improvements, Boston's graduation rate still lags 8 percentage points behind the state's average of 89.3%.
Significant gains were made by English learners and students with disabilities, with increases of nearly 5% and over 5% respectively.

title: Bruins’ playoff hopes are destined to fail if they can’t solve confounding road woes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins struggle on the road with an 11-14-5 record, impacting their playoff prospects.
Pavel Zacha's hat trick wasn't enough in a 5-4 overtime loss against the Penguins.
Bruins hold the second wild-card spot in the East with 76 points, facing tough road schedule ahead.

title: This Marblehead man is running the Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brian Wishart, 42, from Marblehead, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his pediatric patients.
Wishart is the Chief of Pediatric Rehabilitation Medicine at Spaulding Rehab, supporting the hospital and adaptive sports programs.
Funds raised aim to integrate Spaulding's research into clinical care and enhance resources for pediatric patients and specialists.

title: 8 children have now died from flu in Mass. this season
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Eight children in Massachusetts have died from the flu this season, according to the state's Department of Public Health.
As of February 28, 298 adults and 8 children in Massachusetts have died from influenza.
38.1% of Massachusetts residents have received the flu vaccine this season, health officials recommend vaccination for everyone over six months.

title: NBA cancels Hawks’ ‘Magic City’ promotion amid backlash following Luke Kornet’s letter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The NBA canceled the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Monday' promotion after concerns from stakeholders and Luke Kornet's viral letter.
The promotion, linked to Atlanta's Magic City strip club, included chicken wings, branded merchandise, and a performance by T.I.
Commissioner Adam Silver stated the decision was made to address concerns from fans, partners, and NBA employees.

title: Shaquille O’Neal had no clue who Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman is
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Shaquille O'Neal expressed unfamiliarity with Celtics guard Baylor Scheierman during an 'Inside the NBA' broadcast.
Baylor Scheierman, a first-round pick in 2024, has played 59 games for the Celtics this season, filling in for Jayson Tatum.
Scheierman averages 4.8 points per game and is shooting 38.9% from 3-point range despite a recent thumb fracture.

title: Healey calls on aviation company operating at Hanscom to cut ties with ICE
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey urges Signature Aviation to cease involvement in ICE deportation flights at Hanscom Field, citing due process concerns.
Signature Aviation, based in Florida, is integral to ICE operations at Hanscom, handling logistics for deportation flights.
Since Trump’s second term began, 132 ICE flights have departed from Hanscom, escalating immigration enforcement at the Bedford airport.

title: Delivery person falls through a shaft outside Boston’s Omni Parker House
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A delivery worker fell 20 feet through a metal grate outside Boston's Omni Parker House on Friday morning.
Boston Inspectional Services issued a building code violation due to rotted brackets causing the grate collapse.
The hotel must hire a structural engineer to inspect vent grates and submit a repair report for approval.

title: Quincy gas station looking for violist to ‘serenade’ customers
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Quincy Gas owner Harvey Kertzman seeks a violist to perform at his gas station on Quincy Avenue.
Kertzman has signs on pumps since last year for a violist, rejecting offers from guitarists.
A retired violist is set to begin performing when weather reaches 65 degrees or warmer.
